response.justification: The candidate shows well over the required 6 years of full-stack experience with sustained TypeScript/Node.js product work, React and MongoDB in production, cloud and container experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker), and current hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work that directly matches an unusual JD requirement. A strong testing culture (TDD, trunk-based development, authored test libraries and a test runner, taught testing courses) fits the quality expectations. Gaps are mostly stack-specific: no explicit Nest.js, Kotlin, Python, or Vitest/Playwright, and much frontend history is Ember rather than React.
strengths: Deep full-stack TypeScript/Node.js experience across multiple production platforms, including high-scale and low-latency systems
Current AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code-generation work, matching the JD's AI agent development requirement
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, authored testing libraries, and taught testing/automation workshops
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience, the primary backend framework in the stack
Kotlin and Python not demonstrated; Java exposure appears limited and dated
React experience is comparatively thin next to extensive Ember.js frontend history

key_factors.factor: AI agent and MCP tooling experience
Missing Nest.js and several secondary stack languages
